description: Baseline: Eta H2-over-TLS steady-state tail latency (oha HTTP/2 keep-alive, c=16 p=16 n=20000, multi-domain 8, median of 3). echo_1k p99 962us, p50 239us, rps 357k.

asi.diagnosis: Gap is the shared H2+TLS steady-state request path: at c=16 p=16, root/user p99 ~1.9-2.4ms, static_1k ~3.6ms (handler file-I/O, off-limits), echo_1k ~0.96ms (cleanest, full body read+write). echo is FASTEST -- so echo_1k p99 (~1ms) is the cleanest primary to optimize; the 1.6-3x vs Go (0.74ms) is real per-request overhead in h2 frame/HPACK + TLS record read/write, not handshake (amortized) and not handler-bound. echo_rps 357k, root 269k, user 255k, static 202k. p50s are ~240-480us.
asi.hypothesis: baseline of H2-over-TLS steady-state tail latency
asi.inherited: H1 TLS session changes already benefit H2 TLS: SSL_SESS_CACHE_OFF, tls_eio buffer reuse, multi-domain HTTPS, caml_enter_blocking_section around SSL_do_handshake.
asi.noise: echo_1k p99 ~926-962us run-to-run (a few %). root/user/static p99 noisier (1.9-3.6ms). rps and p50 are cleaner corroboration.
asi.first_levers: (1) TLS record write coalescing/batching across concurrent streams (many small TLS records per loop turn under p=16); (2) H2 HEADERS+DATA single writev for fixed responses; (3) HPACK encode/decode alloc reuse; (4) per-connection bookkeeping contention under many streams.
